# openapi-java-client

OpenID Federation Entity Registry API

- API version: 0.5.10-SNAPSHOT

- Build date: 2025-12-03T23:15:26.199430+01:00[Europe/Stockholm]

- Generator version: 7.12.0

REST API for managing OpenID Federation entities, policies, modules, and trust marks. This API provides endpoints for
creating, reading, updating, and deleting federation entities, policies, trust anchors, resolvers, trust marks, and
trust mark subjects.

For more information, please visit [https://www.swedenconnect.se](https://www.swedenconnect.se)

*Automatically generated by the [OpenAPI Generator](https://openapi-generator.tech)*

## Requirements

Building the API client library requires:

1. Java 1.8+
2. Maven/Gradle

## Installation

To install the API client library to your local Maven repository, simply execute:

```shell
mvn clean install
```

To deploy it to a remote Maven repository instead, configure the settings of the repository and execute:

```shell
mvn clean deploy
```

Refer to the [OSSRH Guide](http://central.sonatype.org/pages/ossrh-guide.html) for more information.

### Maven users

Add this dependency to your project's POM:

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.openapitools</groupId>
  <artifactId>openapi-java-client</artifactId>
  <version>0.5.10-SNAPSHOT</version>
  <scope>compile</scope>
</dependency>
```

### Gradle users

Add this dependency to your project's build file:

```groovy
compile "org.openapitools:openapi-java-client:0.5.10-SNAPSHOT"
```

### Others

At first generate the JAR by executing:

```shell
mvn clean package
```

Then manually install the following JARs:

- `target/openapi-java-client-0.5.10-SNAPSHOT.jar`
- `target/lib/*.jar`

## Getting Started

Please follow the [installation](#installation) instruction and execute the following Java code:

```java

import se.swedenconnect.oidf.registry.*;
import se.swedenconnect.oidf.registry.auth.*;
import se.swedenconnect.oidf.registry.api.model.*;
import se.swedenconnect.oidf.registry.api.EntitiesApi;

public class EntitiesApiExample {

    public static void main(String[] args) {
        ApiClient defaultClient = Configuration.getDefaultApiClient();
        defaultClient.setBasePath("http://localhost:8010");
        
        // Configure API key authorization: selectedOrgNumber
        ApiKeyAuth selectedOrgNumber = (ApiKeyAuth) defaultClient.getAuthentication("selectedOrgNumber");
        selectedOrgNumber.setApiKey("YOUR API KEY");
        // Uncomment the following line to set a prefix for the API key, e.g. "Token" (defaults to null)
        //selectedOrgNumber.setApiKeyPrefix("Token");

        // Configure HTTP bearer authorization: bearerAuth
        HttpBearerAuth bearerAuth = (HttpBearerAuth) defaultClient.getAuthentication("bearerAuth");
        bearerAuth.setBearerToken("BEARER TOKEN");

        EntitiesApi apiInstance = new EntitiesApi(defaultClient);
        FederationEntityInput federationEntityInput = new FederationEntityInput(); // FederationEntityInput | 
        try {
            FederationEntity result = apiInstance.createFederationEntity(federationEntityInput);
            System.out.println(result);
        } catch (ApiException e) {
            System.err.println("Exception when calling EntitiesApi#createFederationEntity");
            System.err.println("Status code: " + e.getCode());
            System.err.println("Reason: " + e.getResponseBody());
            System.err.println("Response headers: " + e.getResponseHeaders());
            e.printStackTrace();
        }
    }
}

```

## Documentation for API Endpoints

All URIs are relative to *http://localhost:8010*

-------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------
 *EntitiesApi*                 | [**createFederationEntity**](docs/EntitiesApi.md#createFederationEntity)                      | **POST** /registry/v1/entities/federation         | Create federation entity with auto-generated ID

## Documentation for Authorization


Authentication schemes defined for the API:
<a id="bearerAuth"></a>
### bearerAuth

- **Type**: HTTP Bearer Token authentication (JWT)

<a id="selectedOrgNumber"></a>
### selectedOrgNumber

- **Type**: API key
- **API key parameter name**: selected-org-number
- **Location**: HTTP header

## Recommendation

It's recommended to create an instance of `ApiClient` per thread in a multithreaded environment to avoid any potential
issues.
